key_parameters.name: Limiting Angular Subtense
key_parameters.significance: Divides small-source viewing from extended-source viewing
key_parameters.application: If source angular subtense ≤ 1.5 mrad, use small-source MPEs

key_parameters.name: Maximum Angular Subtense
key_parameters.significance: Angular limit beyond which extended source MPEs are expressed as constant radiance
key_parameters.application: For sources larger than 100 mrad, radiance becomes constant

key_parameters.name: Photochemical Time Constant
key_parameters.significance: Exposure duration at which thermal MPEs are replaced by photochemical MPEs
key_parameters.application: For retinal exposures in 400-600 nm range

key_parameters.name: Thermal Time Constant
key_parameters.significance: Exposure duration beyond which extended-source thermal MPEs become constant irradiance
key_parameters.application: Function of α; determines when MPE transitions to constant value

key_parameters.name: Melanin Correction Factor
key_parameters.purpose: Increases MPE values based on reduced melanin absorption properties
key_parameters.application: Accounts for reduced absorption in skin and retinal pigment epithelium

key_parameters.name: Photochemical Hazard Correction Factor
key_parameters.purpose: Increases MPE values due to reduced photochemical hazards at red end of visible spectrum

key_parameters.name: Pre-retinal Absorption Correction Factor
key_parameters.purpose: Increases MPE values due to pre-retinal absorption
key_parameters.application: Accounts for ocular media absorption before retina

key_parameters.name: Extended Source Correction Factor
key_parameters.purpose: Used for calculating extended-source MPE from small-source MPE
key_parameters.condition: When laser source subtends visual angle exceeding αmin

key_parameters.name: Repetitive Pulse Correction Factor
key_parameters.purpose: Reduces MPE for repetitive-pulse exposure of the eye

key_parameters.name: Limiting Aperture
key_parameters.purpose: Maximum diameter for averaging radiance and radiant exposure
key_parameters.application: For hazard evaluation and classification measurements

steps: Identify applicable spectral region (retinal hazard, corneal hazard, skin)
Measure or calculate angular subtense α of source
If α ≤ αmin, use small-source MPE
If αmin < α ≤ αmax, apply CE correction factor
If α > αmax, use constant radiance MPE
Take most restrictive of: single-pulse limit, CP-corrected limit, or average power limit

aperture_sizes.3.5mm: For older standards or specific conditions

distance_requirements.NOHD: Nominal Ocular Hazard Distance - beyond this distance, irradiance < MPE
distance_requirements.NHZ: Nominal Hazard Zone - space where radiation exceeds MPE

time_bases.0.25s: Aversion response time for visible lasers
time_bases.10s: Standard evaluation time for many applications

UV_A.hazard: Lens focusing possible, some retinal hazard

retinal_hazard.note: Eye's focusing mechanism concentrates energy on retina
